mode: LIVE agent loop with per-turn routing (real tool execution, errors propagate)
findings: all_haiku is OPTIMAL on this easy task: reward 1.0 at $0.32 (~89% cheaper than all_opus).
light_haiku (static per-turn light/heavy switching) is the WORST policy: 5x costlier than all_haiku AND lower reward (0.85). Mixing two models' divergent solving paths mid-trajectory hurts.
escalate degenerates to all_opus because one early error escalates the whole rest of the run.
Routability is strongly task-difficulty dependent: opposite conclusion to the hard-task american-fd L3 (where Haiku could not do the decisive step).
Surface 'this step looks simple' inference is NOT reliable — proven by light_haiku underperforming.
